design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Frontend (React/nginx) and Admin backend (Express) both deployed to Railway
- Auto-deploys triggered on push to GitHub repo
- judgemyprompt.com domain pointed at Railway frontend service
- Frontend nginx proxies /api/* to backend via Railway internal networking
- Deep analysis feature works end-to-end (frontend → backend → AI provider)
- No reliance on user's local machine being online

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Frontend service runs on Railway, serves React SPA
- [ ] Backend service runs on Railway, handles /api/analyze requests
- [ ] Frontend /api/* requests proxy correctly to backend internal URL
- [ ] Push to GitHub master triggers auto-deploy of both services
- [ ] judgemyprompt.com resolves to Railway frontend
- [ ] Heuristic evaluation works (client-side, no backend needed)
- [ ] Deep analysis works when user provides API key
- [ ] Site stays within Railway free tier ($5/mo) for low traffic

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Railway URLs in application code (use nginx config and env vars)
- ❌ NO merging frontend and backend into single service (keep clean separation)
- ❌ NO Cloudflare tunnel in Railway deployment (Railway provides public URLs)
- ❌ NO storing API keys server-side (privacy-first: users provide their own keys)
- ❌ NO changes to the heuristic evaluation logic (it's client-side and works fine)

## Approach
Deploy two Railway services from the same GitHub repo. Each service uses its existing Dockerfile. The frontend nginx.conf is updated to proxy /api/* to the backend's Railway internal hostname instead of Docker Compose's `admin` DNS name. Railway config (railway.json or per-service settings) tells each service which Dockerfile to build. Environment variables (ADMIN_SECRET, CORS_ORIGIN, NODE_ENV) set in Railway dashboard. Domain judgemyprompt.com pointed at Railway via CNAME.

## Architecture
- Railway Project: "Prompt Strength" with 2 services
- Frontend service: builds from frontend/Dockerfile, exposes port 80, public domain
- Backend service: builds from admin/Dockerfile, exposes port 3001, internal only
- Internal networking: frontend nginx proxies to backend via Railway private domain
- Auto-deploy: Railway watches GitHub repo master branch

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Site currently runs in Docker on user's local machine via Cloudflare tunnel. If machine is off, site is down. Railway provides free hosting with auto-deploy from GitHub.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- frontend/Dockerfile: multi-stage build, nginx serves static assets
- admin/Dockerfile: multi-stage build, Node Express API
- frontend/nginx.conf: proxies /api/* to `http://admin:3001` (Docker Compose hostname)
- frontend/src/services/deepAnalysis.ts:50 - only backend call is POST /api/analyze
- usePromptEvaluation.ts - has embedded default levers, no backend fetch needed for heuristics

**Key insight:** The only runtime dependency on the backend is deep analysis. All heuristic evaluation is client-side with embedded defaults.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Frontend + Backend as two Railway services ✓
**What it is:** Deploy both existing Docker containers to Railway as separate services in one project. Update nginx proxy to use Railway internal networking.

**Pros:** Clean separation preserved, both Dockerfiles work as-is, minimal changes
**Cons:** Two services use more of free tier budget

**Chosen because:** Preserves existing architecture, minimal code changes, deep analysis works

#### 2. Frontend only, refactor deep analysis to call APIs from browser ❌
**Why we looked at this:** Would be simpler (one service) and cheaper
**Investigation:** Many AI providers block browser CORS. Would need significant refactoring.
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** CORS issues with provider APIs, significant code changes
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** All target AI providers add CORS support

#### 3. Single service merging Express + static files ❌
**Why we looked at this:** One service = simpler, cheaper
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Breaks clean frontend/backend separation, couples deployments
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Free tier budget becomes a real constraint

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Railway deployment, nginx proxy update, domain setup, auto-deploy
**Out of scope:** Admin frontend deployment (not needed for public site), changing evaluation logic, adding new features
title: Deploy Prompt Strength to Railway

design:
## Goal
Create Railway project, connect GitHub repo, configure two services (frontend + backend), set environment variables, and point judgemyprompt.com at Railway.

## Context
Completed prompt-strength-bz1: nginx.conf.template uses BACKEND_HOST/BACKEND_PORT env vars, Dockerfile uses official nginx template mechanism, backend has /api/health endpoint.

## Steps (Railway Dashboard)

### 1. Create Railway Project
- Go to railway.app → New Project → Deploy from GitHub repo
- Select the Prompt Strength repo
- Railway will auto-detect the Dockerfile and create one service

### 2. Configure Frontend Service
- Rename the auto-created service to "frontend"
- Settings → Root Directory: leave empty (Dockerfile uses build context from root)
- Settings → Dockerfile Path: `frontend/Dockerfile`
- Settings → Watch Paths: `frontend/**`, `shared/**` (only redeploy when these change)
- Variables tab:
  - `BACKEND_HOST` = `backend.railway.internal` (Railway internal DNS for the backend service)
  - `BACKEND_PORT` = `3001`
- Networking → Generate Domain (or add custom domain)
- Health Check: `/health`

### 3. Create Backend Service
- Click "+ New" → GitHub Repo → same repo
- Rename to "backend"
- Settings → Root Directory: leave empty
- Settings → Dockerfile Path: `admin/Dockerfile`
- Settings → Watch Paths: `admin/**`, `shared/**`
- Variables tab:
  - `NODE_ENV` = `production`
  - `PORT` = `3001`
  - `CORS_ORIGIN` = `https://judgemyprompt.com`
  - `ADMIN_SECRET` = (generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`)
- Networking → DO NOT add public domain (internal only)
- Enable Private Networking (so frontend can reach it)
- Health Check: `/api/health`

### 4. Point judgemyprompt.com at Railway
- In Railway frontend service → Settings → Networking → Custom Domain → add `judgemyprompt.com`
- Railway will give you a CNAME target (e.g., `xxx.up.railway.app`)
- In your domain registrar's DNS settings:
  - Delete/update existing CNAME or A record for judgemyprompt.com
  - Add CNAME record: `judgemyprompt.com` → Railway's CNAME target
  - If root domain, some registrars need ALIAS/ANAME record instead of CNAME

### 5. Verify
- Push a commit to trigger deploy
- Check Railway logs for both services
- Visit judgemyprompt.com — SPA should load
- Test deep analysis with an API key — should proxy to backend

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Both services deploy on push to master
- [ ] Frontend accessible at judgemyprompt.com
- [ ] Backend internal only (no public URL)
- [ ] /api/analyze proxies correctly from frontend to backend
- [ ] Health checks passing for both services
title: Set up Railway project with two services and domain

design:
## Goal
Create Railway configuration so both services deploy from the same repo, and update nginx to proxy to the backend via Railway internal networking.

## Implementation

### 1. Update nginx.conf to use templated backend host

Convert `frontend/nginx.conf` to `frontend/nginx.conf.template`:
- Replace `http://admin:3001` with `http://${BACKEND_HOST}:${BACKEND_PORT}`
- **CRITICAL:** Keep all nginx variables ($uri, $host, etc.) as-is — envsubst must only target our variables

### 2. Update frontend Dockerfile for envsubst at runtime

Add to frontend/Dockerfile:
- Set default env vars: `BACKEND_HOST=admin`, `BACKEND_PORT=3001`
- Replace CMD with: `envsubst '${BACKEND_HOST} ${BACKEND_PORT}' < /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf.template > /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf && nginx -g 'daemon off;'`
- **CRITICAL:** The envsubst command MUST specify exact variable names to avoid blanking $uri, $host, etc.

### 3. Update docker-compose.yml for backward compat

- Add `BACKEND_HOST=admin` and `BACKEND_PORT=3001` environment variables to frontend service
- Existing docker-compose continues working with Docker DNS

### 4. Set proxy timeouts for deep analysis

- Add `proxy_read_timeout 120s` and `proxy_connect_timeout 10s` to nginx /api/ location block
- AI provider calls can take 30-60+ seconds; default 60s timeout is too tight

### 5. Add backend health endpoint

- Check if backend has a health endpoint; if not, add GET /api/health returning 200
- Railway needs this for health checks on the backend service

### 6. Files to create/modify

- `frontend/nginx.conf` → Rename to `frontend/nginx.conf.template`, replace admin hostname with `${BACKEND_HOST}:${BACKEND_PORT}`
- `frontend/Dockerfile` → Add envsubst runtime step, set default env vars
- `docker-compose.yml` → Add BACKEND_HOST/BACKEND_PORT env vars to frontend service
- `admin/src/index.ts` → Add /api/health endpoint if missing

## Corner Cases (SRE)
- envsubst without variable list blanks nginx $uri/$host → MUST use `envsubst '${BACKEND_HOST} ${BACKEND_PORT}'`
- BACKEND_HOST not set → defaults to `admin` (docker-compose compat)
- Backend not started when frontend starts → 502 on /api/*, acceptable for Railway (retries on refresh)
- AI calls timing out → proxy_read_timeout 120s handles long-running analysis
- Backend must be internal-only on Railway (no public domain assigned)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] envsubst only replaces BACKEND_HOST and BACKEND_PORT (not nginx vars like $uri)
- [ ] Frontend Dockerfile builds and serves SPA with templated nginx config
- [ ] nginx proxies /api/* to configurable backend host
- [ ] proxy_read_timeout set to 120s for deep analysis calls
- [ ] docker-compose.yml still works locally (backward compatible, BACKEND_HOST=admin)
- [ ] Backend has /api/health endpoint for Railway health checks
- [ ] Both Dockerfiles build successfully
notes: COMPLETED: nginx.conf.template created with envsubst variables and proxy timeouts, Dockerfile updated to use official nginx template mechanism, docker-compose.yml updated with BACKEND_HOST/PORT, /api/health added to backend.
title: Add Railway configuration and update nginx proxy
